concepts.name: Isotopes and atomic number
concepts.definition: Isotopes have the same atomic number, but different mass numbers.

concepts.name: Isobars and atomic number
concepts.definition: Isobars have different atomic numbers, but the same mass number.

concepts.name: Nuclear model of the atom
concepts.definition: The nuclear model of the atom proposes that the nucleus is the central part of the atom, and electrons revolve around it.

chapter_title: Chapter 2 Is Matter Around Us Pure
subject: science

concepts.name: Matter around us is not pure
concepts.definition: Matter around us is a mixture of different substances

concepts.name: Definition of a mixture
concepts.definition: A mixture is a physical combination of two or more substances

concepts.name: Types of mixtures
concepts.definition: Mixtures can be classified into homogeneous and heterogeneous mixtures

concepts.name: Homogeneous mixtures
concepts.definition: A homogeneous mixture is a mixture in which the composition is uniform throughout

concepts.name: Heterogeneous mixtures
concepts.definition: A heterogeneous mixture is a mixture in which the composition is not uniform throughout

concepts.name: Solution
concepts.definition: A solution is a homogeneous mixture of two or more substances

concepts.name: Concentration of a solution
concepts.definition: The concentration of a solution is the amount of solute present per unit volume or per unit mass of the solution

concepts.name: Saturated solution
concepts.definition: A saturated solution is a solution that has dissolved as much solute as it is capable of dissolving

concepts.name: Suspension
concepts.definition: A suspension is a heterogeneous mixture in which the solute particles do not dissolve but remain suspended throughout the bulk of the medium

concepts.name: Colloidal solution
concepts.definition: A colloidal solution is a heterogeneous mixture in which the particle size is too small to be seen with the naked eye

concepts.name: Elements
concepts.definition: An element is a form of matter that cannot be broken down by chemical reactions into simpler substances

concepts.name: Compounds
concepts.definition: A compound is a substance composed of two or more different types of elements, chemically combined in a fixed proportion

concepts.name: Properties of a compound
concepts.definition: The properties of a compound are different from its constituent elements

concepts.name: Physical and chemical changes
concepts.definition: Physical changes occur without a change in composition and no change in the chemical nature of the substance

concepts.name: Chemical changes
concepts.definition: Chemical changes bring change in the chemical properties of matter and we get new substances

concepts.name: Mixtures and compounds
concepts.definition: Mixtures and compounds are substances that can be separated into their constituent elements or compounds

concepts.name: Separation techniques
concepts.definition: Separation techniques are used to separate the constituent elements or compounds of a mixture or compound

concepts.name: Solution: solvent and solute
concepts.definition: A solution is a homogeneous mixture of a solvent and a solute

concepts.name: Concentration of a solution
concepts.definition: The concentration of a solution is the amount of solute present per unit volume or per unit mass of the solution

concepts.name: Solubility
concepts.definition: Solubility is the amount of solute that can be dissolved in a given amount of solvent at a given temperature

concepts.name: Atoms
concepts.definition: The smallest particle of an element that cannot usually exist independently and retain all its chemical properties.

concepts.name: Molecules
concepts.definition: The smallest particle of an element or a compound that is capable of an independent existence and shows all the properties of that substance.

concepts.name: Atomic Mass
concepts.definition: The mass of an atom of an element, expressed in atomic mass units (u).

concepts.name: Molecular Mass
concepts.definition: The sum of the atomic masses of all the atoms in a molecule of a substance.

concepts.name: Formula Unit Mass
concepts.definition: The sum of the atomic masses of all the atoms in a formula unit of a compound.

concepts.name: Law of Conservation of Mass
concepts.definition: The sum of the masses of the reactants and products remains unchanged during a chemical reaction.

concepts.name: Law of Definite Proportions
concepts.definition: Elements are always present in a definite proportion by mass in a pure chemical compound.

concepts.name: Chemical Formula
concepts.definition: A symbolic representation of the composition of a compound.

concepts.name: Polyatomic Ions
concepts.definition: Clusters of atoms that act as an ion and carry a fixed charge.

concepts.name: Valency
concepts.definition: The combining power of an element, which determines how it will combine with other elements to form a compound.

concepts.name: Atomicity
concepts.definition: The number of atoms constituting a molecule.

concepts.name: Relative Atomic Mass
concepts.definition: The average mass of an atom of an element, compared to 1/12th the mass of one carbon-12 atom.

concepts.name: Atomic Radius
concepts.definition: The distance from the centre of an atom to its outermost electron.

concepts.name: Molecular Mass Unit
concepts.definition: A unit of mass used to express the molecular mass of a substance.

concepts.name: Formula Unit
concepts.definition: A group of atoms that form a compound, often represented by a chemical formula.

concepts.name: Chemical Formulae
concepts.definition: A way of representing the composition of a compound using symbols and valencies.

concepts.name: Polyatomic Ion
concepts.definition: A cluster of atoms that acts as an ion and carries a fixed charge.

concepts.name: Atomic Mass Unit
concepts.definition: A unit of mass used to express the atomic mass of an element.

concepts.name: Molecular Mass Unit
concepts.definition: A unit of mass used to express the molecular mass of a substance.

concepts.name: Relative Formula Mass
concepts.definition: The average mass of a formula unit of a compound, compared to 1/12th the mass of one carbon-12 atom.

chapter_title: Chapter 5 The Fundamental Unit of Life
subject: science

concepts.name: Cell Theory
concepts.definition: The cell theory states that all living organisms are composed of cells, and that cells are the basic units of life.

concepts.name: Cell Structure
concepts.definition: The cell structure consists of a plasma membrane, cytoplasm, nucleus, and various organelles.

concepts.name: Cell Functions
concepts.definition: Cells perform various functions such as respiration, nutrition, and waste removal.

concepts.name: Cell Division
concepts.definition: Cell division is the process by which new cells are formed.

concepts.name: Mitosis
concepts.definition: Mitosis is the process of cell division that results in two identical daughter cells.

concepts.name: Meiosis
concepts.definition: Meiosis is the process of cell division that results in four new cells with half the number of chromosomes.

concepts.name: Plasma Membrane
concepts.definition: The plasma membrane is the outermost layer of the cell that separates the cell from its environment.

concepts.name: Cytoplasm
concepts.definition: Cytoplasm is the fluid content inside the plasma membrane.

concepts.name: Nucleus
concepts.definition: The nucleus is the control center of the cell that contains DNA and directs the life processes of the cell.

concepts.name: Endoplasmic Reticulum
concepts.definition: The endoplasmic reticulum is a network of membrane-bound tubes and sheets that functions in the transport and synthesis of proteins.

concepts.name: Golgi Apparatus
concepts.definition: The Golgi apparatus is a complex of membrane-bound vesicles that functions in the storage, modification, and packaging of substances.

concepts.name: Lysosomes
concepts.definition: Lysosomes are membrane-bound sacs that contain digestive enzymes and function in the breakdown of cellular waste.

concepts.name: Mitochondria
concepts.definition: Mitochondria are membrane-bound organelles that generate energy for the cell in the form of ATP.

concepts.name: Plastids
concepts.definition: Plastids are membrane-bound organelles that are responsible for photosynthesis and storage in plant cells.

concepts.name: Vacuoles
concepts.definition: Vacuoles are membrane-bound organelles that are responsible for storage and waste removal in plant cells.

concepts.name: Osmosis
concepts.definition: Osmosis is the movement of water molecules across a selectively permeable membrane.

concepts.name: Cell Wall
concepts.definition: The cell wall is a rigid outer covering of plant cells that provides structural support.

concepts.name: Prokaryotic Cell
concepts.definition: A prokaryotic cell is a cell that lacks a true nucleus and membrane-bound organelles.

concepts.name: Eukaryotic Cell
concepts.definition: A eukaryotic cell is a cell that has a true nucleus and membrane-bound organelles.

concepts.name: Cell Division Types
concepts.definition: There are two types of cell division: mitosis and meiosis.

concepts.name: Chromoplasts
concepts.definition: Chromoplasts are membrane-bound organelles responsible for photosynthesis in plant cells.

concepts.name: Leucoplasts
concepts.definition: Leucoplasts are membrane-bound organelles responsible for storage in plant cells.

concepts.name: Vacuole Functions
concepts.definition: Vacuoles are responsible for storage and waste removal in plant cells.
